school: Companions of the Prophet — one of the most important transmitters of hadith; authority on the Prophet's private life and character
quotes: Acquire knowledge before you become a leader — for once you lead, you will find no time to learn.
The people most severely tested are the prophets, then those nearest to them, then those nearest to them.
Whoever tells you that the Prophet concealed part of what was revealed to him is a liar.
Strange is the case of a believer — there is good for him in everything.

school: Cousin of the Prophet — the Prophet prayed for him 'O God, teach him the interpretation'; father of Quranic exegesis; 1,696 hadiths transmitted
parents: al-'Abbas ibn 'Abd al-Muttalib / Umm Fadl Lubaba bint al-Harith
children: 'Ali bin 'Abdullah bin 'Abbas , 'Abbas bin 'Abdullah bin al-Abbas , Muhammad bin 'Abdullah bin 'Abbas , 'Ubaydallah bin 'Abdullah bin al-Abbas , Al-Fadl, Lubabah, Asma
quotes: Take knowledge wherever it comes from — but do not accept the actions of men as religion until you see them in the Quran or Sunnah.
The most beloved of deeds to God is the one that is consistent, even if it is small.
If you want God to give you what you ask, then ask often — for God loves the persistent caller.
Take knowledge wherever it comes from — but do not accept the actions of men as religion until you see them in the Quran or Sunnah.
The most beloved of deeds to God is the one that is consistent, even if it is small.
If you want God to give you what you ask, ask often — God loves the persistent caller.

school: Youngest daughter of the Prophet Muhammad and Khadijah bint Khuwaylid; wife of Ali ibn Abi Talib and mother of Hasan, Husayn, Zaynab and Umm Kulthum — the sole surviving line through which the Prophet's descendants continue to this day. The Prophet declared her the Sayyidat Nisāʾ al-Janna (foremost woman of Paradise) and rose to greet her whenever she entered a room. She died only six months after her father in 632 CE, still mourning his loss, and was the first of his family to follow him in death.

books.title: Fatimah al-Zahra — World History Encyclopedia
books.note: Modern scholarly overview of her life and significance

quotes: One who worships God sincerely — God will fulfil his needs and relieve his poverty from a source he could never expect.
The best things for women are that they should not see men and men should not see them.
